evaluator_output.reason: The $20 budget constraint is tight but yields 1 high-quality option (Truthear HOLA at $19) that directly matches the user's intent for best sound quality at this price point — results are acceptable.

final_response:
# Your Sub-$20 Sound Quality Winner

Meet the **Truthear HOLA** at **$19**—and yes, you read that right. For basically a twenty-dollar bill, you're getting a wired in-ear with a 10mm dynamic driver tuned to the Harman Target curve, which is the gold standard for neutral, accurate sound. Think of it as the headphone world's best-kept secret: audiophile-grade tuning without the audiophile price tag.

The liquid crystal polymer diaphragm handles everything from clean bass extension to smooth treble without any harshness. It's genuinely impressive for the price point, whether you're using it in a studio setting or traveling.

**The honest bit:** At this price point, there's really only one standout option in the catalog—and you've found it. If you wanted to explore more alternatives, I'd suggest relaxing the budget slightly (even $30–40 opens up more choices) or letting me know if wireless capability matters to you. But if you're set on staying under $20, the HOLA is legitimately your move.
